Grastidium australie, also known as the Australian Grasswort, is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.

The bioactive constituents of Grastidium australie include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Grastidium australie include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds.

Grastidium igneum is native to Australia and is found in various regions, including Western Australia and the Northern Territory.
